I think one of the things that I hear a lot is like it's just SEO on steroids.
that how you all think of it or is there more kind of granular details than that?
I think our perspective, I mean, there's obviously like best practices around how you structure the content.
It's going to be like LLM friendly, you know, with the amount of, you know, H1s, H2s, H3s, like using tables in the content, bringing statistics, cross-linking, keeping content fresh.
This is definitely like more of a benefit for AEO.
I think at the core, we believe that if you take this audience first approach and if you really focus on what are all the jobs to be done, right?
Like not just the keyword analysis of what are your competitors targeting that you're not?
What are the untapped, like some rush keywords?
But more so, like, how can you create content that's the best answer to your audience's questions at scale?
If you can do that, and then also if you can, like, create content more quickly, you can win an AI search.
Because right now, like, things are getting cited that are not factual.
Things are getting cited that are done by, like, brands that have good authority, publish consistently, have, you know, unique information, perspectives.
But also, like, really, like, well-researched facts that they're bringing into their content.
And so I think, like, this is why, like, our deep researchers, like, a really critical piece to what we're creating.
Is, like, we are scanning a ridiculous amount of sources.
And, like, we're only pulling things in that we can validate with our fact checker after the fact.
And so, like, being able to do deep research that's so much more unique than, like, what someone would be.
able to do with a custom GPT, with asking Claude to, you know, generate research for like a specific brief.
That is, it allows us to like drive visibility a lot faster.
